What happens to pending income tax proce: The old Income Tax Act 1961 has been replaced by the new Income Tax Act 2025 from April 1, 2025. As a result, all pending income tax proceedings under the old Act will be automatically transferred to the new Act. This means that any income tax proceedings that were pending as of April 1, 2025, will now be governed by the new Act. It is important to note that the new Act has different timelines, procedures, and requirements compared to the old Act. Therefore, it is crucial to review and update your income tax returns, claims, and appeals to ensure compliance with the new Act.
What is the new section number for TDS o: The new section number for TDS on salary under Income Tax Act 2025 is 192A.
How does the Tax Year concept affect adv: Advance tax is calculated on the basis of the Tax Year. For example, if the Tax Year is 2023-24, then the Advance tax payable for FY 2024-25 will be calculated on the basis of the income earned in the AY 2023-24. Similarly, the Advance tax payable for FY 2025-26 will be calculated on the basis of the income earned in the AY 2024-25. Therefore, it is important to ensure that the income is reported in the AY 2023-24 for the purpose of calculating Advance tax for FY 2024-25. Similarly, income reported in the AY 2024-25 will be considered for calculating Advance tax for FY 2025-26.
What is faceless assessment under Income: Faceless assessment is a new feature introduced in the Income Tax Act 2025. It is a simplified process for taxpayers to file their return of income and pay income tax without having to appear in person at the Income Tax Department. Under this system, taxpayers can file their return of income online and pay income tax using their bank account or credit card. The Income Tax Department will then process the return and tax payment without requiring any physical presence of the taxpayer. This feature is available for Individual, HUF, AOP, BOI, and Artificial Juridical Person taxpayers.
Can I still use old Income Tax Act 1961: Yes, you can still use the Income Tax Act 1961 terminology in your return for FY 2025-26. However, it is recommended to use the new Income Tax Act 2025 terminology for better understanding and compliance.
